document.title: Risk Management Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz GT1717C and GT1717CP digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk acceptability criteria, risk control, residual risk evaluation, and production/post-production information for the device.
keyPoints: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
The report references EU MDR, Korean medical device law, ISO 14971, ISO 13485, IEC 60601-1, IEC 62304, IEC 62366-1, and related guidance.
Before risk control, 2 risks were in the acceptable region and 47 risks were in the unacceptable region.
After risk control, all 49 risks were evaluated in the acceptable region.

Production and post-production information is collected through customer satisfaction, complaints, inspections, measuring device monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Risk management plan scope
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities, identifying and describing the medical device and the life cycle phases for which each element of the plan is applicable

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The GT1717C/GT1717CP is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator. User misuse may occur depending on the manufacturer's maintenance responsibilities and modes.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance. - Acquisition image is obtained as intended. - The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank.

concepts.name: Risk control option analysis
concepts.evidence: We used one or more of the following risk control options in priority order listed below, for the risks need to be reduced to an acceptable level

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Refer to A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The document is a Rev.0 risk management report dated May 25, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-RMR-CYN (Rev.0) ... Prepared by Dongchul Seo R&D team May 25, 2025 ... Revision No. 0 Rev. Date May 25, 2025 Revision Description Establishment

evidence.claim: The product models covered are GT1717C and GT1717CP.
The product is a digital flat panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The expected service life is stated as 5 years.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The patient population excludes infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient population Adult and children except infants and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants and pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.

evidence.claim: Applicable national and regional regulations listed are EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 ... Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2019 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: The risk analysis process includes intended use and misuse, safety-related characteristics, hazard identification, and risk estimation.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk analysis process consists of the following steps ... description of the intended use ... identification of the characteristics ... identification of hazards and hazardous situations ... estimation of risks for each hazardous situation

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria define risk levels 1 to 4 as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Before risk control, the report lists 2 acceptable risks and 47 unacceptable risks, total 49.
Risk reduction is required for risks in the Red Zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk reduction is required for the risks that belong to the Red Zone.

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 49 risks are in the acceptable region and 0 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results after risk control ... EN ISO 14971:2019 49 0 49

evidence.claim: The report concludes that overall residual risks are acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Overall residual risk posed by the medical device are all acceptable.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information collection includes customer satisfaction surveys, complaint statistics, inspection monitoring, measuring device monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality Assurance 1. Customer satisfaction survey ... 2. Statistical processing of customer complaints ... 3. Monitoring results of the incoming inspection and end product test results ... 4. Monitoring the measuring devices ... R&D 1. Design changes ... 2. Monitoring the national and international standards ... 3. Collect and analysis of the market information ... Production Process management monitoring ... Management Monitoring the purchaser assessment results

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting and reviewing production and post-production information and managing quality problems.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: The Quality manager shall report safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency if necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document text lists European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and does not identify FDA as an authority.
Expected service life is inconsistent: one section states 'Expected service life 5 years' while IEC 60601-1 clause 4.4 states service life is 'approximatelyTBD'.

Risk count evidence is conflicting: the analysis states '43 risks were identified and estimated' but tables show 2 acceptable plus 47 unacceptable risks before control and 49 total risks after control.
The document contains apparent text quality or transcription issues such as 'GT1717CPis', 'approximatelyTBD', 'thepatient', and 'EN 15322-1' where related references list EN 15223-1.
Some model evidence contains a likely typo, 'GT1717CW/GT1717CP', while the title page and product tables identify GT1717C and GT1717CP.
The document states no battery is applied to GT1717C/GT1717CP, but the overall residual risk section mentions 'using certified battery' as a control measure.
